name: Dave Valentin
abstract: David Peter Valentin (April 29, 1952 – March 8, 2017) was an American Latin jazz flautist of Puerto Rican descent.

interviews.title: Dave Valentin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

quotes.text: Yeah he played on some of the records, great, great, great, great man. In fact we just did that remake for the “West Side Story” also, where it was just piano and flute. And we did “I Feel Pretty” but with a whole new twist to it, which eventually had an Afro-Cuban thing but in seven-four. So it’s [scats]. And we did it at Carnegie Hall, we did that. And there’s a lull just before he starts his piano solo and he’s ready to go, and people start applauding. And they’re applauding, they’re applauding. So Dave gets up and says “the © Fillius Jazz Archive -11-

quotes.text: That’s Carnegie Hall, man. Tuxedos. “We no finni ye.”
quotes.caption: Vague recollection of tuxedos and a phrase at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Josiah Gluck
about_quotes.evidence: I hadn’t had a lot of live experience other than doing a live record with the flutist Dave Valentin at the Blue Note a few months before.

about_quotes.speaker: Allan Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah I know. Well you’ve got one of the greatest Latin players in the business, Dave Valentin. And boy what a monster he is man.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I look back at people like Dave Valentin and McLean – Jackie McLean’s son, whom I taught in New York
